events.description:
Join us for an incredible night of good vibes and heavy grooves as we bring our unique style of high desert reggae, rock, and groove music to Heber City Market

The Last Light is a reggae based groove collective composed of 7 artists who call the Southern Utah Desert home. With a lineup of players each with their own deep musical resumes and variety of styles to bring to the table, they've set out to create a brand new sound blending reggae, rock, hip-hop, & funk together into an electric sound. 

They Released their Debut EP, "Music Is The Medicine" in 2023 and have performed shows in Utah, Nevada, California, & Florida in the last 4 years. Their newest single “I’ll Be Ok” was released 2/27/26.

Always with the crowd in mind, they bring all of their energy to their performances with one singular goal - to get you on your feet, out of your mind, and into the music. Bringing full spectrum vibes that mirror a complete human experience - The Last Light's three vocalists, Charlie, Kaleb, and Eli will bring you lyrics that make you feel, lyrics that make you laugh, and together they bring thick grooves that make you dance....

events.title: Wasatch History Speaker Series
events.description:
Wasatch County Library invites the community to attend the next Wasatch History Speaker Series presentation on Wednesday, June 10, from 6–7 PM.

History educators Dawain Wheatley and Easton Powell will explore the creation of the Declaration of Independence, the ideals that shaped the nation, and the document's continuing relevance as the United States approaches its 250th anniversary. The presentation will examine how the Declaration's principles have influenced communities throughout American history, including Wasatch County.

This FREE event is part of the library's America250-inspired local history series, which commemorates 250 years of United States history, culture, and community stories.

Seating is available on a first-come, first-served basis....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description:
Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date.

A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere.

Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Longevity, Simplified: An Expert-Led Conversation
events.description:
Black Rock Mountain Resort’s Onyx Club is partnering with local wellness experts for its June Speaker Series event, *Longevity, Simplified: What Actually Moves the Needle for Your Health*, to focus on the practical application of long-term health optimization. The enlightening event, which is free and open to the community, will take place on Wednesday, June 24, from 5:30 to 7:00 p.m. at Black Rock Mountain Resort in Heber City, Utah.

The event brings together two respected, local leaders in longevity, health, and performance: Dr. Timothy Duerler of Ovation Private Health and Connor Darnbrough, co-founder of The Smart Fit Method.

Designed to cut through the noise surrounding wellness trends and longevity advice, the discussion will focus on the four pillars of Longevity — sleep, exercise, nutrition, and stress management — and how individuals can apply evidence-based strategies to improve daily performance, vitality, and long-term health outcomes. Participants are encouraged to attend with questions for the Q&A to follow the brief, informative presentations.

Attendees will gain insight into:

The lifestyle factors that have the greatest impact on longevity and overall health

How advanced diagnostics such as lab testing, body composition analysis, and VO₂ max assessments can personalize wellness strategies

Which health metrics matter most for optimizing performance and extending health span

Practical ways to implement sustainable habits within demanding, real-world schedules

Darnbrough will share the story behind The Smart Fit Method, a science-driven training system developed through his own experience overcoming severe injuries and rebuilding strength after a professional skiing career. The approach, embraced by everyone from elite athletes looking to boost their performance to seniors prioritizing their healthspan, emphasizes efficient, sustainable training designed to improve resilience, recovery, and long-term physical performance.

events.title: Utah Symphony
events.description: We're bringing a symphonic celebration of America's 250th birthday to Utah's Little Switzerland! Enjoy a performance by our musicians against green backdrops and crisp alpine air, right in the heart of Midway's Town Square. The program has a patriotic flair, featuring favorite classical works, Broadway selections, American folk music, and more, including: Works from celebrated American composers like Aaron Copland and Leroy Anderson, including John Williams' Liberty Fanfare. Western-inspired pieces \(perfect for iconic Western vistas\)-Elie Siegmeister's Western Suite and Aaron Copland's "Hoe-Down" from Rodeo. Western Suite was first recorded with the Utah Symphony under Maurice Abravanel in 1971. Iconic classical works like the beautiful Adagietto from Mahler's Symphony No. 5, a Maurice Abravanel favorite and first recorded by the Utah Symphony in 1975. Performances by violinist Aubree Oliverson, a Utah native and recent Masterworks soloist for the Utah Symphony. Soprano Amy Owens \(former Utah Opera Resident Artist\) performs "Glitter and Be Gay" from Bernstein's Candide, as well as "Quando m'en vo" from Puccini's La bohème and Ricky Ian Gordon's "Simple Child" from The Grapes of Wrath. These pieces honor Utah's world-class arts legacy: La bohème was Utah Opera's first production in 1978 and The Grapes of Wrath was co-commissioned and premiered in 2007....

events.title: Rigby Summer
events.description:
Formerly touring as summerland, Oklahoma-based songwriter, Rigby Summer, weaves an Americana soundscape that reflects the road she’s traveled, from her Kansas City jazz upbringing to the beach pop of her former home in southern California to the red dirt roots of her current home in Stillwater, Oklahoma. While her influences range from Mary Chapin Carpenter to The Frames, Sia Furler to Johnny Cash, her sound often draws comparisons to Ingrid Michaelson and Peggy Lee—a little modern, a little old-fashioned, always earnest.

In 2017, after a five-year hiatus from performing music, she founded the Monday@Modella songwriter pop-up and the ALL MUSIC STARTS LOCAL initiative and started sharpening her musical teeth, slowly carving a niche for herself in the Oklahoma scene. Since then, she has had the privilege of sharing the stage with and learning from a myriad of local and touring artists, including Bill Scorzari, Belle Plaine, Blake Berglund, Heather Styka and many more. She was also given honorable mention in the 2017 Woody Guthrie Festival Songwriting Contest and was a semi-finalist in the 2019 Wyoming Singer-Songwriter Contest, in which she was voted as the audience choice winner for her round.

Hher first full-length album “Geography” was released in October 21 and broke the top ten on the NACC Folk Chart in November 2021....

events.title: Debra Fotheringham
events.description:
Debra Fotheringham is a seasoned singer-songwriter, guitarist, and percussionist whose voice has long been a quietly powerful force in the Utah music scene. Based in Salt Lake City, she’s spent over two decades crafting songs that balance soul-baring vulnerability with refined musicianship. With three studio albums already under her belt, Debra released her fourth this October—her most ambitious and daring work to date.

A founding member of the beloved roots/gospel collective The Lower Lights, Debra’s voice carries both the warmth of tradition and the spark of innovation. Her haunting vocal work has been featured on television networks like PBS, AMC, and The CW, and she’s a recurring collaborator with electronic music luminary Kaskade, contributing to multiple albums including his Grammy-nominated Atmosphere.

Equal parts storyteller and sonic explorer, Debra Fotheringham continues to shape a career that bridges genres and generations—always rooted in authenticity, always reaching for something deeper....

events.title: Music Elevated State Tour
events.description: We’re bringing a symphonic celebration of America’s 250th birthday to Utah’s Little Switzerland! Enjoy a performance by our musicians against green backdrops and crisp alpine air, right in the heart of Midway’s Town Square. The program has a patriotic flair, featuring favorite classical works, Broadway selections, American folk music, and more, including: - Works from celebrated American composers like Aaron Copland and Leroy Anderson, including John Williams’ Liberty Fanfare. Western-inspired pieces \(perfect for iconic Western vistas\)—Elie Siegmeister’s Western Suite and Aaron Copland’s “Hoe-Down” from Rodeo. Western Suite was first recorded with the Utah Symphony under Maurice Abravanel in 1971. - Iconic classical works like the beautiful Adagietto from Mahler’s Symphony No. 5, a Maurice Abravanel favorite and first recorded by the Utah Symphony in 1975. - Performances by violinist Aubree Oliverson, a Utah native and recent Masterworks soloist for the Utah Symphony. - Soprano Amy Owens \(former Utah Opera Resident Artist\) performs “Glitter and Be Gay” from Bernstein’s Candide, as well as “Quando m’en vo” from Puccini’s La bohème and Ricky Ian Gordon’s “Simple Child” from The Grapes of Wrath. These pieces honor Utah’s world-class arts legacy: La bohème was Utah Opera’s first production in 1978 and The Grapes of Wrath was co-commissioned and premiered in 2007...
events.location: Midway Music on the Square, Midway, UT

events.title: Sleepover Under The Stars: "Divine Creatrix"
events.description:
What if the rest & replenishment you crave is closer than you think? 🏕️🔥🌿🌸No flight. No week off. No elaborate logistics. Just you, a bag, and one night to remember what it feels like to be fully alive & connected in sisterhood community — closer than you think.

Step into the river with Wild Women Tribe this July 11-12 for our first Sleepover Under the Stars camping experience just 45 minutes from Park City.

"Divine Creatrix" is an elemental immersion in the Uinta Mountains. You’ll move through the sacred languages of earth, water, and fire — cold plunging in the river, gathering around a ceremony as the stars come out, sleeping beneath a sky so full of stars it resets something in you. Co-led and held lovingly by founder Renee Huang, yogi, reiki master & sound healer Rachel Smoot, and somatic breathwork facilitator Jilana McIntyre.

You’ll leave with more than you arrived with. Not just rest. A return.

🌿A nervous system that finally exhaled

🌿Clarity you’ve been reaching for

🌿Energy that actually lasts

🌿The memory of your own stillness

🌿Sisterhood that holds you

🌿A felt sense of coming home

Join us for a 24-hour portal where you just need to bring your overnight bag and your dreams. Save $99 when you pair it with our Aug 1 sleepover “Creatures Great & Small,” for a sweet & grounding campout with the rescued farm animals at Sage Mountain Sanctuary in Kamas.

Space for up to 16 women each event -- first come, first served. Deposit saves your spot.

DETAILS: wildwomentribe.net/sleepover

REGISTER: wildwomentribe.net/registration

VENMO: venmo.com/wildwomentribe (last 4 digits 9583 -- do not select purchase protection otherwise you'll be asked to cover the fee that is deducted). $250 deposit; $349 per woman per event -- save $99 when you register for both July 11 and Aug 1 ($598 total)...
events.location: Uinta National Forest, Kamas, UT
events.address: trailhead to be sent upon registration

events.title: Sleepover Under The Stars: "Creatures Great & Small"
events.description:
Step into the serenity of the farm with Wild Women Tribe this August 1-2 for our second Sleepover Under the Stars camping experience just 45 minutes from Park City. 🏕️🔥🌿🌸No flight. No week off. No elaborate logistics. Just you, a bag, and one night to remember what it feels like to be fully alive & connected in sisterhood community — closer than you think.

Some of the most profound teachers have four legs and no agenda. They meet you with soft eyes, an unhurried presence, and an unconditional acceptance that most of us have forgotten to offer ourselves.

**Creatures Great & Small** unfolds at Sage Mountain Animal Sanctuary — one of the most quietly magical places within 20 minutes of Park City. You’ll connect with the rescued animals, guided by Sage Mountain Sanctuary co-founder Lauren Lockey, and feel what happens when a living creature simply accepts you, exactly as you are. Includes vegan dinner and breakfast. Co-led and held lovingly by founder Renee Huang, yogi & founder Lauren Lockey.

You’ll leave with more than you arrived with. Not just rest. A return.

🌿A nervous system that finally exhaled

🌿Clarity you’ve been reaching for

🌿Energy that actually lasts

🌿The memory of your own stillness

🌿Sisterhood that holds you

🌿A felt sense of coming home

Join us for a 24-hour portal where you just need to bring your overnight bag and your dreams. Save $99 when you pair it with our July 11 sleepover “Divine Creatrix,” for a sweet & grounding riverside campout along Mirror Lake Highway in the Uintas.

Space for up to 16 women each event -- first come, first served. Deposit saves your spot.

events.description:

Kids can enjoy a fun discussion, share their favorite story choices, and discover history in a unique and interactive way. For questions or more information, contact the Kids Book Club facilitator, Ms. Brittnie, or call the library at 435-654-1511.

events.title: Park City Film Presents: A Dog’s Way Home (Kamas)
events.description:
As a puppy, Bella finds her way into the arms of Lucas, a young man who gives her a good home. When Bella becomes separated from Lucas, she soon finds herself on an epic 400-mile journey to reunite with her beloved owner. Along the way, the lost but spirited dog touches the lives of an orphaned mountain lion, a down-on-his-luck veteran and some friendly strangers who happen to cross her path.

***Based on the book of the same name by W. Bruce Cameron.*** 

Join us under the stars in Kamas for a free film screening with free food trucks! Presented with Spanish Subtitles.

**Event Details:**

Outdoor film screening at the Kamas City Park (505 S Main St, Kamas, UT 84036)

Free Food Trucks on site from 5-7pm, movie at 7pm (with Spanish subtitles)

Blankets and lawn chairs welcome

In partnership with the Kamas Valley Community Foundation, we are accepting donations of non-perishable, unexpired food items to benefit the Kamas and Oakley Food Pantries. ***Presented in partnership with Summit County Health***...
